dependsOn.guidance: Briefs that must be built before this one — usually the tables this model's relationships point at.

instructions.whoFillsThisIn: Developers (Product can sanity-check the information being stored).
instructions.when: Whenever a page needs to store data. One of these per model/table.
instructions.how: Answer in plain English where you can.
instructions.note: This replaces the earlier idea of storing scenarios as work items of type 'test'. The QA Test Designer brief left that choice to build time; a dedicated model won because a scenario must be able to point at either a Deliverable or a Work Item, and parentItemId can only express the second.

whatThisModelIs.question: What does this model represent, in one line?
whatThisModelIs.answer: A plain-English test scenario QA designs in the Test area, optionally associated with a Deliverable or a Work Item.

fields.guidance: One entry per piece of data.

entries.description: The Product this test case belongs to
What is being tested, in a few words
The scenario in plain English (given / when / then welcome, not required)
Whether a real test exists for this scenario yet

entries.rules: set when the state is implemented
must reference a Deliverable row when set
entries.description: The Deliverable this test is associated with, if any

entries.rules: must reference a work item row when set
entries.description: The Work Item this test is associated with, if any
When the test case was written
When the test case last changed

relationships.guidance: How this model connects to others.

lookupsAndPerformance.question: Which fields do we search or filter on a lot?

retention.question: How long do we keep this data / when is it deleted?
retention.answer: Kept until QA deletes the test case. Deleting the Deliverable or Work Item it points at only unlinks it — the test itself survives, because it is still worth running.

sensitiveFields.question: Any sensitive fields that need extra protection?

invariants.question: Anything that must always be true?
invariants.answer: A test case always belongs to an existing Product and always has a title; state is 'designed' or 'implemented'; deliverableId and workItemId, when set, reference rows that exist.

changesOverTime.guidance: Each time you come back to change this model, add an entry describing what should change.
